response:
[
  {
    "vc_id": 42643,
    "reference": "Gen 18:23",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The wicked are those whose moral status is the opposite of the righteous — Abraham's question implies that wickedness and righteousness are distinct inner conditions that ought to receive distinct judgments.",
    "keywords": ["moral contrast", "guilt objective", "judgment deserved", "conscience appealing", "justice moral"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42644,
    "reference": "Gen 18:25",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The wicked are those who deserve death as a just consequence of their condition — conflating them with the righteous would violate the divine judge's moral character.",
    "keywords": ["moral contrast", "guilt objective", "judgment deserved", "justice moral", "will divine"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42698,
    "reference": "Exo 2:13",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The man in the wrong is the aggressor in the fight — his wickedness is an active behavioral choice to strike a companion, a relational violation observed and named by an outsider.",
    "keywords": ["aggression active", "relational violation", "guilt behavioral", "conscience named", "will acting"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42699,
    "reference": "Exo 9:27",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "Pharaoh acknowledges being in the wrong in contrast to God's rightness — this is a moment of conscience surfacing under pressure, even if the admission is situational rather than transformative.",

  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42645,
    "reference": "Exo 23:1",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The wicked man recruits others into complicity through false testimony — wickedness here is a social and moral force that corrupts the will of bystanders drawn into alliance with it.",
    "keywords": ["complicity invited", "will corrupted", "social contagion", "false witness", "alliance evil"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42646,
    "reference": "Exo 23:7",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The wicked are those God will not acquit — their guilt is objective and permanent before divine justice, standing in contrast to the innocent whom must be protected.",
    "keywords": ["guilt objective", "divine judgment", "acquittal denied", "moral contrast", "justice unrelenting"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1676,
    "reference": "Lev 18:22",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "A specific sexual act is declared an abomination — the term names a category-violation that evokes divine revulsion, marking the act as fundamentally disordered against the created order.",
    "keywords": ["order violated", "divine revulsion", "boundary transgressed", "act defiling", "category violation"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65922,
    "reference": "Lev 18:26",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "The abominations are a defined set of practices that the community — native and stranger alike — must entirely abstain from, making avoidance of them an act of obedience to divine statutes.",
    "keywords": ["will refusing", "obedience required", "boundary communal", "defilement avoided", "statute keeping"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65923,
    "reference": "Lev 18:27",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "The prior inhabitants committed all these abominations and thereby defiled the land itself — the acts carry a polluting power that extends beyond the individual to the moral state of the created environment.",
    "keywords": ["defilement spreading", "land polluted", "act defiling", "corruption habitual", "moral contamination"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65924,
    "reference": "Lev 18:29",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Performing any of these abominable acts results in being cut off from the community — the penalty names the social and covenantal consequence of acts that violate the divine order.",
    "keywords": ["act defiling", "excision covenantal", "consequence enacted", "boundary violated", "community severed"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65925,
    "reference": "Lev 18:30",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "These abominable customs are practices that make the self unclean — keeping the divine charge means refusing to let these patterns from the past take hold in one's own behavior.",
    "keywords": ["self defiled", "will refusing", "custom resisted", "purity guarded", "defilement avoided"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65926,
    "reference": "Lev 20:13",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Both parties in the act are said to have committed an abomination — guilt is mutual and the act itself is the abomination, carrying a death penalty that signals its gravity before God.",
    "keywords": ["act defiling", "guilt mutual", "divine revulsion", "boundary transgressed", "penalty ultimate"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65894,
    "reference": "Num 16:26",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The wicked men's sins have a contagious power — proximity to them risks being swept away, so the community must actively distance itself to preserve moral and physical safety.",
    "keywords": ["social contagion", "sin spreading", "will withdrawing", "moral danger", "community contaminated"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1682,
    "reference": "Deu 7:25",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Idols of foreign gods are an abomination to God — coveting what is on them ensnares the will, drawing a person into what God utterly detests.",
    "keywords": ["divine revulsion", "will ensnared", "covetousness warned", "idol abhorred", "desire corrupting"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 7:26",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Bringing an abominable thing into one's household transfers its devoted-to-destruction status — the abomination has a contaminating power that re-orders the will of anyone who tolerates it.",
    "keywords": ["defilement transferred", "will corrupted", "household contaminated", "abhorrence required", "devotion destroyed"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1683,
    "reference": "Deu 12:31",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "The abominable things the nations do for their gods — including child sacrifice — are what God hates; their worship is an inner and ritual corruption that distorts the will toward what is most detestable.",
    "keywords": ["divine hatred", "will distorted", "worship corrupted", "ritual abomination", "moral inversion"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 13:14",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "The abomination here is an act of communal apostasy requiring diligent investigation before judgment — its gravity demands certainty before the community responds.",
    "keywords": ["apostasy communal", "act defiling", "investigation required", "judgment pending", "truth discerned"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 1684,
    "reference": "Deu 17:1",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Offering a flawed animal is an abomination — the inner act of presenting something defective to God reveals a disordered valuation of God's worth, treating worship carelessly.",
    "keywords": ["worship defiled", "valuation disordered", "divine revulsion", "offering rejected", "heart careless"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65930,
    "reference": "Deu 17:4",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "An abomination done in the community requires diligent inquiry before punishment — the term signals an act so serious that its truth must be verified before the community acts.",
    "keywords": ["act defiling", "community violated", "truth verified", "judgment warranted", "investigation diligent"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65931,
    "reference": "Deu 18:9",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "The nations' practices are abominable — Israel is warned not to learn or imitate them, meaning the will must actively resist adopting patterns God detests.",
    "keywords": ["will refusing", "imitation resisted", "practices abhorred", "divine revulsion", "boundary cultural"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 18:12",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Whoever practices divination and occult arts becomes personally an abomination to God — the repeated acts are the reason God is expelling the nations, making the practitioner repellent to the divine.",
    "keywords": ["practitioner abhorred", "divine revulsion", "habitual practice", "expulsion deserved", "occult defiling"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 20:18",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Idolatrous practices are abominable and contagious — allowing the nations to teach them risks corrupting Israel's will to sin against God.",
    "keywords": ["will corrupted", "sin learned", "abomination contagious", "divine revulsion", "teaching dangerous"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65933,
    "reference": "Deu 22:5",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Cross-dressing is declared an abomination — the act violates a divinely ordered distinction between male and female, making the one who does it repellent to God.",
    "keywords": ["order violated", "divine revulsion", "boundary transgressed", "act defiling", "distinction erased"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 65934,
    "reference": "Deu 23:18",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Bringing a prostitute's earnings or a male prostitute's fee into God's house is an abomination — it profanes the sacred space by mixing what is detestable with what is holy.",
    "keywords": ["sacred profaned", "divine revulsion", "defilement introduced", "holiness violated", "act abhorred"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 24:4",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Remarrying a defiled former wife is an abomination before God — the act would sin against the moral-covenantal order and bring guilt upon the land.",
    "keywords": ["defilement relational", "moral order violated", "land polluted", "covenant abomination", "act prohibited"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42722,
    "reference": "Deu 25:1",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "In a legal dispute the judge is to condemn the guilty party — the wickedness is a legally and morally adjudicated status that must be named and punished to uphold justice.",

  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 25:2",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The guilty man's wickedness is measured and proportionate punishment is assigned — the inner moral status translates directly into a just and bounded legal consequence.",
    "keywords": ["guilt measured", "punishment proportionate", "justice enacted", "legal consequence", "wickedness bounded"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 25:16",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Dishonest commercial practice — false weights and measures — makes a person an abomination to God, linking moral character directly to the quality of one's daily transactions.",

  },
  {

    "meaning": "A secretly made and set-up idol is an abomination to God — the secrecy reveals that the heart knows the act is wrong even while the will proceeds with it.",

  },
  {
    "vc_id": 38439,
    "reference": "Deu 28:20",
    "strongs": "H7455",
    "meaning": "The evil of deeds is the moral quality of actions that flows from forsaking God — the corrupt inner disposition toward God produces a corrupted will that acts against him, bringing divine judgment.",

  },
  {

    "strongs": "H8251",
    "meaning": "The detestable idols of surrounding nations are objects that Israel has directly witnessed — the memory of seeing them is a warning against internalizing the reverence others gave them.",
    "keywords": ["idol detestable", "memory warning", "perception alerting", "will guarded", "revulsion evoked"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "Deu 32:16",
    "strongs": "H8441",
    "meaning": "Worshipping abominations provokes God to anger and jealousy — the inner dynamic is a relational betrayal where the people's will turns to what is detestable, injuring the divine-human bond.",
    "keywords": ["betrayal relational", "divine jealousy", "will turning", "worship corrupted", "anger provoked"]
  },
  {
    "vc_id": 42647,
    "reference": "1Sa 2:9",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The wicked are those who will be silenced in darkness — their wickedness is an inner condition that ultimately cannot sustain itself, cut off by God's sovereign power.",
    "keywords": ["wickedness futile", "judgment inevitable", "darkness moral", "will overturned", "divine sovereignty"]
  },
  {

    "reference": "1Sa 15:23",
    "strongs": "H0205G",
    "meaning": "Presumption and self-assertion — here called iniquity — are treated as equivalent to idolatry; the inner trouble is a will that elevates its own judgment above the divine word.",
    "keywords": ["presumption inward", "will self asserting", "idolatry equated", "word rejected", "heart exalting"]
  },
  {

    "strongs": "H7455",
    "meaning": "Eliab accuses David of evil in the heart — projecting an inner disposition of pride and presumption onto his motives, locating the evil specifically in the heart as its seat.",
    "keywords": ["heart evil", "pride accused", "inner disposition", "motive imputed", "heart located"]
  },
  {

    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The proverb asserts that wickedness is the natural and inevitable fruit of a wicked inner source — the wicked person is constitutionally oriented toward producing wicked action.",

  },
  {

    "reference": "2Sa 4:11",

    "meaning": "Wicked men who kill a righteous man bear a blood-guilt that demands divine retribution — wickedness here is an act of moral violence that places its perpetrators under irrevocable judgment.",

  {

    "strongs": "H8251",
    "meaning": "The foreign deity Milcom is labeled an abomination — the term names the detestable character of a false god that draws the heart away from covenant loyalty.",

  },

    "strongs": "H8251",

[
  {

  },

  },

    "meaning": "The wicked cannot experience peace; their inner state is characterised by an irreducible restlessness that is the natural consequence of their moral orientation away from God.",

  },
  {

    "reference": "Isa 53:9",
    "strongs": "H7563",
    "meaning": "The servant is buried among the wicked despite having no inner moral corruption — no violence in will, no deceit in speech — marking an absolute contrast between his inner purity and the wickedness that surrounds him.",
